title: TaskFlow — Collaborative Task Management Platform
description: A real-time collaborative task management platform for distributed engineering teams.
background: Engineering teams at fast-growing startups struggle to coordinate work across time zones. Existing tools lack deep integration with code review workflows and sprint planning, causing context switching and productivity loss.
architecture: TaskFlow is built on a serverless event-driven architecture deployed on AWS. The frontend is a React single-page application served via CloudFront and communicates with a GraphQL API backed by AWS AppSync. Business logic lives in Node.js Lambda functions organized by domain (tasks, projects, notifications, users). Persistent state is stored in DynamoDB with a global secondary index strategy for flexible query patterns. Real-time collaboration is powered by AppSync subscriptions over WebSocket. Authentication is handled by Amazon Cognito with JWT-based authorization. All infrastructure is defined as code using AWS CDK. CI/CD pipelines run on GitHub Actions with automated testing gates before every deployment.

inScope: Task creation, assignment, and lifecycle management
Real-time collaborative updates and presence indicators
Sprint planning with drag-and-drop board interface
GitHub pull request integration for task status updates
assumptions: Users have modern browser support (Chrome 90+, Firefox 88+, Safari 14+)
Teams are expected to have fewer than 500 members per organization

goals.title: Reduce cross-team coordination overhead
goals.description: Enable distributed engineering teams to coordinate work without synchronous standups by providing a shared, always-current view of project state.
successCriteria: Average time to assign and scope a task drops below 5 minutes
Weekly standup duration reduced by at least 30% within 60 days of adoption

goals.title: Deliver real-time collaborative editing without merge conflicts
goals.description: Provide instant, conflict-free updates to task descriptions, acceptance criteria, and comments so that multiple team members can contribute simultaneously.
successCriteria: Concurrent edits by two users on the same task resolve deterministically within 200ms
Zero data-loss incidents reported during the first 90 days post-launch

goals.title: Seamlessly integrate with existing GitHub workflows
goals.description: Automatically reflect pull request status changes on linked tasks, closing the gap between code review and project management without manual updates.
successCriteria: Pull request webhook events update task status within 10 seconds
At least 80% of implementation tasks have a linked PR within 48 hours of starting

requirements.title: Task lifecycle management with status transitions
requirements.description: Users must be able to create tasks, set priority and assignee, and transition tasks through defined statuses (backlog, in-progress, review, done). Status transitions must be validated against a configurable workflow.

acceptanceCriteria.given: A project member is viewing the task board
acceptanceCriteria.when: They create a new task with a title, description, and assignee
acceptanceCriteria.then: The task appears in the backlog column and the assignee receives an in-app notification

requirements.title: Real-time presence and live update propagation
requirements.description: All connected clients must receive task updates within 500ms of a change being committed. The UI must show which team members are currently viewing or editing each task.

acceptanceCriteria.given: Two users have the same task open in their browsers
acceptanceCriteria.when: One user updates the task description
acceptanceCriteria.then: The other user's browser reflects the new description within 500ms without a page refresh

requirements.title: GitHub webhook integration for linked pull requests
requirements.description: The system must accept GitHub webhook events for pull request opened, merged, and closed actions, and update the status of any task linked to that pull request accordingly.

acceptanceCriteria.given: A task has a linked GitHub pull request
acceptanceCriteria.when: The pull request is merged on GitHub
acceptanceCriteria.then: The linked task transitions to the review status within 15 seconds

nonFunctionalRequirements.description: The GraphQL API must respond to 95th-percentile queries within 200ms under a sustained load of 500 concurrent users.
nonFunctionalRequirements.measurementMethod: Load test with k6, 10-minute sustained ramp, measured at API Gateway

guardrails.description: All user-supplied input must be validated and sanitized server-side before persistence to prevent injection attacks and data corruption.
guardrails.rationale: Client-side validation can be bypassed; server-side enforcement is the last line of defense.
guardrails.consequence: Unvalidated input reaching the database could lead to data corruption, XSS vulnerabilities, or unauthorized access.

techStack.name: React
techStack.rationale: Mature ecosystem, strong TypeScript support, and team familiarity reduce onboarding friction.

techStack.name: AWS DynamoDB
techStack.rationale: Serverless, pay-per-request pricing aligns with early-stage traffic patterns, and single-table design enables the required query flexibility.

folderStructures.description: Top-level directory layout for the TaskFlow monorepo.
folderStructures.content:
taskflow/
├── apps/
│   ├── web/          # React SPA
│   └── api/          # Lambda function handlers
├── packages/
│   ├── core/         # Shared domain types and utilities
│   └── ui/           # Design system components
├── infrastructure/   # AWS CDK stacks
├── scripts/          # Developer tooling
└── .github/
    └── workflows/    # CI/CD pipeline definitions

acceptanceCriteria.given: A team member with project access is authenticated
acceptanceCriteria.when: They navigate to the project board and create a task
acceptanceCriteria.then: The task is persisted, appears on the board in real-time for all connected members, and the assignee is notified

epics.title: Core Task Management Domain
epics.description: Implement the foundational task entity model, CRUD operations, and status transition engine that all other epics and features depend upon.
epics.objective: Deliver a fully functional task domain layer including persistence, validation, and status-machine enforcement so downstream epics can build on a stable foundation.
epics.architecture: The task domain is implemented as a set of Lambda resolvers connected to AppSync. Each resolver validates input against a Zod schema, applies business rules through a pure-function state machine, and persists to DynamoDB using a single-table design. The Task entity uses a composite primary key (PK: TASK#<taskId>, SK: METADATA) with GSIs for project-scoped queries (GSI1PK: PROJECT#<projectId>) and assignee queries (GSI2PK: USER#<userId>). Optimistic locking via a version attribute prevents lost updates under concurrent writes. Lambda functions share a core package that exports domain types and the state machine to ensure consistency across resolvers.

inScope: Task entity CRUD operations via AppSync resolvers
Status transition validation via configurable state machine
DynamoDB single-table schema and GSI definitions
Optimistic locking to prevent concurrent write conflicts

goals.title: Provide a stable, tested task domain foundation for all subsequent epics
goals.description: All task CRUD operations and state transitions are implemented, tested, and stable before other epics build on top of them.
successCriteria: Unit test coverage for domain logic exceeds 90%
All four CRUD operations pass integration tests against a live DynamoDB table

acceptanceCriteria.given: An authenticated user with project membership
acceptanceCriteria.when: They call the createTask mutation with valid input
acceptanceCriteria.then: A task record is created in DynamoDB, the response includes the new task ID, and the status is set to backlog

apiContracts.name: Task CRUD Mutations
apiContracts.description: createTask, updateTask, deleteTask, and transitionTaskStatus mutations with input validation and authorization guards.

sharedPatterns.name: DynamoDB single-table item factory
sharedPatterns.description: A factory function that constructs DynamoDB PutItem payloads with correct PK/SK/GSI key patterns for each entity type, ensuring consistent key formatting across all Lambda handlers.

tickets.title: Define Task domain type and DynamoDB schema
tickets.description: Create the core Task TypeScript type, Zod validation schema, and DynamoDB single-table key patterns including GSI definitions for project and assignee queries.

implementationSteps.description: Create packages/core/src/domain/task.ts with the Task TypeScript type and TaskStatus enum.
Add the Zod TaskSchema with field-level validation rules matching the domain type.
Define DynamoDB key helper functions: taskPK, taskSK, taskGSI1PK, taskGSI2PK.

guardrails: All exported types must be derived from the Zod schema using z.infer<> to prevent type drift between runtime validation and compile-time types.

codeReferences.description: Zod schema defining the complete Task entity shape and validation rules.

typeReferences.description: Core Task domain type inferred from TaskSchema; used by all Lambda resolvers.

blueprintReferences.context: The status field on the Task type must match the states defined in the state machine blueprint.

Reference implementation pattern for tkt-1-1, snippet 2.

tickets.title: Implement createTask and updateTask Lambda resolvers
tickets.description: Build the AppSync Lambda resolvers for createTask and updateTask mutations, using the Task schema from tkt-1-1 for input validation and DynamoDB PutItem/UpdateItem for persistence.

acceptanceCriteria.given: An authenticated user calls the createTask GraphQL mutation
acceptanceCriteria.when: All required fields are provided and valid
acceptanceCriteria.then: A new task is created in DynamoDB and the resolver returns the created task with its generated ID

implementationSteps.description: Create apps/api/src/resolvers/task/create-task.ts implementing the createTask mutation resolver.
Create apps/api/src/resolvers/task/update-task.ts implementing the updateTask mutation resolver with optimistic locking.
Wire both resolvers into the AppSync schema by updating the resolver mapping configuration.

guardrails: Input must be validated through TaskSchema.parse() before any DynamoDB write to ensure invalid data never reaches the database.

codeReferences.description: AppSync Lambda resolver handler for the createTask GraphQL mutation.

typeReferences.description: Domain type used to type-check resolver return values and DynamoDB payloads.

blueprintReferences.context: Newly created tasks must be initialized in the backlog state as shown in the state machine blueprint.
Resolvers connect to DynamoDB via the Lambda layer depicted in the architecture blueprint.

codeSnippets.description: Reference implementation pattern for tkt-1-2, snippet 1.
Reference implementation pattern for tkt-1-2, snippet 2.

tickets.title: Implement task status transition state machine
tickets.description: Implement a pure-function state machine that enforces allowed status transitions for tasks, returning descriptive errors for invalid transitions.

acceptanceCriteria.given: A task is in in_progress status
acceptanceCriteria.when: The transitionTaskStatus mutation is called with target status review
acceptanceCriteria.then: The transition is accepted and the task status is updated to review

implementationSteps.description: Create packages/core/src/domain/task-state-machine.ts with the allowed transitions map.
Implement the transition() pure function that validates the transition and returns the new status or throws a domain error.
Export the state machine from the core package public API.

guardrails: The state machine must be a pure function with no side effects so it can be used safely in both Lambda resolvers and unit tests without mocking.

codeReferences.description: Pure function that validates and applies a task status transition, throwing a domain error on invalid transitions.

typeReferences.description: Union type of all valid task status values, used as input and return type for the transition function.

blueprintReferences.context: The allowed transitions map is derived directly from the state machine blueprint edges.

codeSnippets.description: Reference implementation pattern for tkt-1-3, snippet 1.

tickets.title: Implement deleteTask resolver and transitionTaskStatus resolver
tickets.description: Build the deleteTask (soft-delete) Lambda resolver and the transitionTaskStatus resolver that uses the state machine from tkt-1-3 to validate and apply transitions.

acceptanceCriteria.when: The deleteTask mutation is called by an authorized user
acceptanceCriteria.then: The task's deletedAt field is set to the current timestamp and it no longer appears in list queries

implementationSteps.description: Create apps/api/src/resolvers/task/delete-task.ts with a soft-delete resolver setting deletedAt.
Create apps/api/src/resolvers/task/transition-task-status.ts that calls the state machine and writes the new status to DynamoDB.
Add resolver mappings for both mutations in the AppSync configuration.

guardrails: Soft delete must use a conditional expression to prevent deleting already-deleted tasks, avoiding accidental data loss on concurrent requests.

codeReferences.description: AppSync Lambda resolver that validates and applies task status transitions using the state machine.

typeReferences.description: Domain type used for typing the resolver response after a soft delete or status transition.

blueprintReferences.context: The transitionTaskStatus resolver must only permit the transitions shown in the state machine blueprint.
Resolvers interact with DynamoDB through the Lambda layer shown in the architecture blueprint.

codeSnippets.description: Reference implementation pattern for tkt-1-4, snippet 1.
Reference implementation pattern for tkt-1-4, snippet 2.

tickets.title: End-to-end integration tests for task CRUD domain
tickets.description: Write integration tests covering all four CRUD operations, valid status transitions, and invalid transition rejection against a real DynamoDB test table.

acceptanceCriteria.given: The test suite runs against a seeded DynamoDB table
acceptanceCriteria.when: All task CRUD integration tests execute
acceptanceCriteria.then: 100% of tests pass and coverage for the domain package exceeds 90%

implementationSteps.description: Create apps/api/src/__tests__/integration/task-crud.test.ts with test cases for create, update, delete, and transition.
Add a DynamoDB table fixture helper that creates and tears down a test table per test suite run.
Configure CI workflow to run integration tests against a local DynamoDB Local container.

qualityGates: All CRUD operations must return correct HTTP-equivalent status codes via AppSync error handling
Invalid status transitions must return a domain error with a human-readable message
Test suite must complete in under 60 seconds on CI

guardrails: Tests must not share DynamoDB table state across test cases; each test must set up and tear down its own data to prevent flaky ordering dependencies.

codeReferences.description: Top-level describe block grouping all integration tests for the task domain.

typeReferences.description: Domain type used to assert response shapes in integration test assertions.

blueprintReferences.context: Integration tests must cover every valid transition edge and at least two invalid transition scenarios from the state machine blueprint.
Tests invoke the Lambda resolvers through the same AppSync-to-Lambda path shown in the architecture blueprint.

blueprints.title: Task Entity State Machine
blueprints.content:
```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
  [*] --> backlog : task created
  backlog --> in_progress : assignee starts work
  in_progress --> review : PR opened
  review --> done : PR merged
  review --> in_progress : PR closed without merge
  done --> [*]
```

blueprints.title: System Architecture Overview
blueprints.content:
```mermaid
graph TD
  Client[React SPA] -->|GraphQL| AppSync
  AppSync --> LambdaTask[Task Lambda]
  AppSync --> LambdaNotif[Notification Lambda]
  LambdaTask --> DynamoDB
  LambdaNotif --> SES
  GitHub -->|Webhook| APIGateway
  APIGateway --> LambdaWebhook[Webhook Lambda]
  LambdaWebhook --> DynamoDB
```
